essay_takeaways: This chunk is exclusively the tail end of the book's glossary (pp. 150–151, entries Y–Z), containing no connected prose, essays, or grammar explanations. All extractable value is in the vocabulary list itself.

High-priority business register cluster: 营业额, 预付金, 子公司, 资产, 折旧, 租赁, 拥有权, 战略, 针对, 优先, 依据 — these recur constantly in financial reports, contracts, and strategy documents. Drill collocations, not just definitions.

Idiom cluster worth memorising as essay openers or transitions: 众所周知 (it is well known that…), 一蹴而就 (often negative: 不能一蹴而就 — cannot be achieved overnight), 忧心忡忡 (to convey stakeholder anxiety), 有识之士 (to confer authority on a viewpoint).

China-specific institutional vocabulary: 职称 (state-system professional-rank titles), 摇号 (lottery allocation of scarce permits), 一二线 (tier-1/2 city market framing) — essential for understanding Chinese business press and policy documents.

Productive suffix to internalize: 一族 (-zú) as a demographic label suffix. Examples from broader corpus: 上班族 (office workers), 月光族 (those who spend their whole salary), 啃老族 (adults dependent on parents). Use it to coin or decode social-group terms instantly.
Tech/digital layer: 云存储, 移动 (as adj. prefix: 移动端, 移动支付, 移动互联网), 指纹 — these appear in any contemporary business-Chinese reading and signal digital-economy fluency.

OCR note: The file shows OCR artifacts — Russian-language watermark text ('Больше книг о китайском языке на') and timestamps ('16:33:03') — but these do not corrupt the Chinese content, which is clean and fully recoverable.
